evals.prompt: Design a Godot 4 third-person RPG market district that introduces a faction conflict, supports a direct route and a risky rooftop route, and can be grayboxed before final art.
evals.expected_output: Defines the player promise, controller-derived metrics, flow and meaningful route differences, a small playable graybox, visual-story handoffs, quest hooks, and a path and performance test matrix.
assertions: The proposal starts from narrative purpose, player verbs, camera, and measured controller metrics
The direct and rooftop routes differ materially and are tested with quest, save, navigation, and exported-build states

evals.prompt: Plan and audit a 2D Godot metroidvania zone built with TileMapLayer. It needs a readable critical path, an ability-gated loop, optional lore, checkpoints, and an art-pass handoff.
evals.expected_output: Maps flow and metrics, assigns TileMapLayer and TileSet responsibilities, keeps interactive state in scenes, and defines navigation, checkpoint, art, story, and playtest gates.
assertions: TileMapLayer, TileSet, scene-owned interactions, camera, collision, and navigation responsibilities are explicit
The gated loop, optional lore, checkpoints, backtracking, and art replacement are verified in motion

evals.prompt: Audit our Godot stealth fortress. Players miss the infiltration choices, guards fail on stairs, one checkpoint traps them behind a locked gate, and the art pass cut performance in half.
evals.expected_output: Separates readability, spatial flow, navigation, state recovery, and measured performance issues, then proposes the smallest graybox and integrated-slice retests.
assertions: Choice readability, navigation connectivity, checkpoint recovery, and performance are diagnosed as distinct evidence tracks
The retest covers routes, stairs, gate states, reload, target hardware, and an exported build

evals.prompt: Turn this approved Godot dungeon graybox into a handoff for a Blender modular kit without losing cover, sightlines, navigation, landmarks, or frame budget.
evals.expected_output: Produces a spatial and visual asset contract with metrics, pivots, grid, variants, collision and navigation assumptions, budgets, acceptance scene, and rollback or cut priorities.
assertions: The handoff preserves measured gameplay geometry and defines an explicit modular asset contract
Representative art is validated in Godot before full replacement of the graybox

evals.prompt: Design the complete branching logic for a murder quest where every suspect can die early and evidence can be destroyed.
evals.expected_output: Should prefer godot-quest-designer because reactive story graph, facts, consequences, and hostile-path narrative testing are primary.

evals.prompt: Implement coyote time, wall sliding, controller vibration, and regression tests in our Godot player controller.
evals.expected_output: Should prefer godot-game-creation-engineer because engine-specific runtime implementation is primary.

evals.prompt: Rebalance our extraction-game rewards so risky objectives outperform safe farming without causing an expert snowball.
evals.expected_output: Should prefer gameplay-consultant because economy and player incentives are primary rather than spatial design.
